description: D3.js SVG timeline renders people (bars) and events (points) on a horizontal time axis spanning -4100 to 110
verification: GET /api/timeline returns items
SVG renders .timeline-item elements
People render as bars, events as points/bars based on date range

description: Timeline supports zoom (0.1x–200x) and pan with mouse/trackpad, including zoom toolbar buttons
verification: Mouse wheel zooms SVG
Click-drag pans SVG
Zoom buttons (#btn-zoom-in, #btn-zoom-out, #btn-fit) work
Scale extent clamped to [0.1, 200]

description: Colored background bands show biblical time periods (Patriarchal, Exodus, Monarchy, Exile, etc.)
verification: GET /api/timeline/periods returns period objects
SVG .layer-periods renders colored rectangles behind items

description: FTS5-powered search across people and events with debounced input (250ms) and dropdown results
verification: GET /api/search?q=moses returns results with snippets
Typing in #search-input triggers debounced API call
Dropdown appears with clickable results

description: Checkboxes toggle visibility of people and events independently
verification: #filter-people and #filter-events checkboxes control includePeople/includeEvents query params
Timeline re-renders with filtered data

description: Radio buttons filter by significance level (all, major, moderate, minor)
verification: Significance radio group sends 'significance' filter to API
GET /api/timeline?significance=major returns only major items

description: Dropdown filters by time period (Patriarchal, United Monarchy, etc.)
verification: #filter-period dropdown populated from /api/timeline/filters
Selection filters timeline items by period

description: Dropdown filters people by role (patriarch, prophet, king, judge, etc.)
verification: #filter-role dropdown populated from /api/timeline/filters
GET /api/timeline?role=prophet returns only prophets

description: Dropdown filters events by category (covenant, military, prophetic, etc.)
verification: #filter-category dropdown populated from /api/timeline/filters
GET /api/timeline?category=covenant returns only covenant events

description: Dropdown filters people by Israelite tribe
verification: #filter-tribe dropdown populated from /api/timeline/filters
GET /api/timeline?tribe=Judah returns only Judah tribe

description: Clear All button resets all filters to default state
verification: Click #btn-clear-filters resets checkboxes, radios, and dropdowns
Timeline reloads with unfiltered data

description: Clicking a person shows right-side panel with dates, role, tribe, significance, description, chronology notes, related events, and scripture references
verification: GET /api/people/{id} returns person detail
#detail-panel opens with person info sections
Related events are clickable links

description: Clicking an event shows detail panel with date, category, significance, description, key figures, locations, and scripture references
verification: GET /api/events/{id} returns event detail
#detail-panel opens with event info sections
Key figures are clickable links

description: Detail panel can be closed via the X button
verification: Click #btn-close-detail hides panel
State.selectedItem resets to null

description: Timeline visually distinguishes date confidence levels (exact, approximate, estimated, traditional) via opacity and dashed borders
verification: Items with lower confidence render with reduced opacity or dashed borders in SVG
Detail panel shows confidence badge

description: Full dark-themed UI with custom CSS variables
verification: styles.css uses --bg-primary, --text-primary etc. with dark color values
All panels, header, and timeline use dark backgrounds

description: Layout adjusts to window resizes — timeline SVG redraws on resize with debounce
verification: window 'resize' event triggers timeline re-render
SVG width/height update to match container

description: Current center year is displayed in the header during zoom/pan
verification: #year-display updates as user pans the timeline
Year format uses BC/AD notation

description: GET /api/timeline returns timeline items with flexible query parameters
verification: Returns 200 with array of TimelineItemDto objects
Supports startYear, endYear, role, category, significance, period, tribe, includePeople, includeEvents

description: GET /api/timeline/periods returns time period definitions for background bands
verification: Returns 200 with array of period objects (name, startYear, endYear, color)

description: GET /api/timeline/books returns list of biblical books with metadata
verification: Returns 200 with array of book objects

description: GET /api/timeline/filters returns available filter option values
verification: Returns object with timePeriods, roles, eventCategories, tribes arrays

description: GET /api/people/{id} returns full person detail with relationships
verification: Returns 200 with person, events, and scripture references
Returns 404 for non-existent ID

description: GET /api/events/{id} returns full event detail with people and locations
verification: Returns 200 with event, people, locations, and scripture references
Returns 404 for non-existent ID

description: GET /api/search?q= performs FTS5 full-text search across people and events
verification: Returns 200 with array of search results (id, name, type, snippet, startYear)
Returns 400 when q is missing or < 2 chars

description: Database auto-creates from embedded Schema.sql and SeedData.sql on first run
verification: DatabaseInitializer reads SQL from assembly embedded resources
Schema creates 10 tables + 2 FTS5 virtual tables + triggers
Seed inserts 47 people, 52 events, 66 books, 15 locations

description: FTS5 virtual tables auto-sync via INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on people and events tables
verification: Schema.sql defines triggers: people_ai, people_au, people_ad, events_ai, events_au, events_ad
Inserting a person row populates people_fts automatically

description: Centralized State object with pub/sub pattern — all modules subscribe to state changes
verification: state.js exposes State.subscribe() and State.updateFilter()
Filter/search changes trigger re-render via state callbacks

description: Only api.js uses fetch() — enforced by architecture test
verification: ArchitectureTests.cs: OnlyApiJs_Uses_Fetch_Directly
Other JS files import Api module instead of calling fetch

description: C# layer rules enforced via architecture tests — Models cannot import Data/Endpoints, Data cannot import Endpoints, Endpoints cannot use SQLite/Dapper directly
verification: ArchitectureTests.cs: 6 layer-boundary tests
Error messages include what's wrong, why, and how to fix

description: Active filter chips display below the filter bar showing all applied filters with remove buttons
verification: Applying a filter shows a chip with the filter name and an X button
Clicking X on a chip removes that filter and reloads the timeline

description: Dual-handle date range slider filters timeline items by year range
verification: Slider allows selecting a start and end year range
Timeline filters to only show items within the selected date range

description: Items outside active filters are dimmed rather than hidden, providing context while emphasizing matches
verification: Filtered-out items render with reduced opacity
Matching items render at full opacity

description: Role and category filters support multi-select via checkbox groups instead of single-select dropdowns
verification: Multiple roles can be checked simultaneously
Multiple event categories can be checked simultaneously
Timeline shows items matching any selected value

description: Date confidence filter allows filtering by confidence level (exact, approximate, estimated, traditional)
verification: Checkbox group for date confidence levels in filter panel
GET /api/timeline supports dateConfidence parameter

description: Scripture book filter allows filtering timeline items by associated biblical book
verification: Dropdown or list of biblical books in filter panel
Selecting a book filters to items with scripture references from that book

description: Location filter allows filtering events by associated geographic location
verification: Location options populated from /api/timeline/filters
Selecting a location filters to events at that location

description: Integration test GetFilters_ReturnsAllFieldsRequiredByFrontend validates filter API returns all required fields
verification: Test asserts timePeriods, roles, eventCategories, tribes arrays are present and non-empty
dotnet test passes with this test included

description: People table has father_id and mother_id columns enabling genealogical tree tracking
verification: Schema.sql defines father_id and mother_id INTEGER columns on people table
Entities.cs Person record includes FatherId and MotherId properties
SeedData.sql contains UPDATE statements linking ~40 family relationships

description: Database seeded with 322 biblical figures spanning all biblical eras from Adam to Phoebe, including Cain, Abel, Melchizedek, Nebuchadnezzar, Cyrus, complete Jesus lineage (Matthew 1 + Luke 3), brothers of Jesus, and the Bethany family
verification: SeedData.sql contains INSERT statements for 322 people
Includes complete Gen 5 + Gen 11 genealogical chain from Adam to Abraham
Includes Father of Lazarus placeholder linking Lazarus, Martha, Mary of Bethany
Each person has associated scripture references and person_relationships entries

description: Events have multiple scripture references showing parallel accounts across Gospels and OT books (e.g., Crucifixion has Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John references)
verification: event_scripture junction table links events to multiple scripture_references
Detail panel displays all parallel references for an event
56 parallel references added covering Gospel synoptics and OT parallels (Kings/Chronicles)

description: Selecting or searching for an item highlights it on the timeline with a white glow border and pulse animation
verification: timeline.js highlightSelected() applies .selected class to matching SVG element
CSS .timeline-item.selected has white stroke, drop-shadow glow, and 3-cycle pulse-glow animation
Highlight updates on every render and selection state change

description: All OT dates aligned to Ussher chronology (Annales Veteris Testamenti, 1650): Creation 4004 BC, Flood 2349 BC, Abraham born 1996 BC, Exodus 1491 BC. Divided Kingdom dates realigned from Thiele to Ussher starting from 975 BC division using Biblical reign lengths. 12 Gen 5/11 genealogical ancestors added. Elijah death_year set to NULL (translated, 2 Kgs 2:11). All date_notes cite Ussher with Anno Mundi references.
verification: Adam birth_year = -4004, death_year = -3074 (930 years, Gen 5:5)
Abraham birth_year = -1996, death_year = -1821 (175 years, Gen 25:7)
Exodus event start_year = -1491 (Ussher AM 2513)
Ahab death_year = -897 (Ussher: 918-897 BC, 22 years, 1 Kgs 16:29)
Hezekiah birth_year = -751, death_year = -697 (Ussher: 726-697 BC, 29 years)
Fall of Samaria = -721 (Ussher AM 3283)
Elijah death_year = NULL (translated, 2 Kgs 2:11)
Elijah on Mount Carmel event = -905 (during Ahab's reign 918-897)
5 new Ussher-dated events: Destruction of Sodom, Covenant of Circumcision, Joseph Made Ruler, Tabernacle Completed, Ark Captured
All 106 tests pass with Ussher dates

description: Complete genealogy of Jesus from both Matthew 1 and Luke 3 added to seed data. 52 people (IDs 270-321): Matthew's royal line, Luke's pre-/post-exilic lines, plus all 4 brothers of Jesus (Mark 6:3). Full unbroken ancestor chain from Jesus to Adam (74 ancestors).
verification: SeedData.sql contains INSERT statements for people IDs 270-321
Matthew line: David → Solomon → ... → Zerubbabel → Abiud → ... → Jacob → Joseph → Jesus
Luke line: David → Nathan → Mattatha → ... → Neri → Shealtiel → Zerubbabel → Rhesa → ... → Heli → Joseph → Jesus
Brothers of Jesus: James (144), Joses (319), Simon (320), Judas/Jude (321) — all with father_id=122, mother_id=38
Jesus lineage API returns 74 ancestors and 4 siblings
Integration test GetLineage_Jesus_FullDavidicLine verifies 35+ ancestors including Adam, Abraham, David, Solomon
All 81 tests pass

description: GET /api/lineage/people returns all people for family tree, GET /api/lineage/{id} returns subject, ancestors, descendants, siblings, and extended family via recursive CTEs
verification: GET /api/lineage/people returns sorted list of LineagePersonDto with parent IDs
GET /api/lineage/{id} returns subject, ancestors, descendants, siblings, and extendedFamily
Recursive CTEs walk father_id/mother_id for multi-generation traversal
ExtendedFamily includes children of ancestors not already in ancestors/descendants/siblings
Siblings query finds people sharing father_id or mother_id with subject
Returns 404 for non-existent person ID
106 unit + integration tests verify endpoints

description: Family Tree tab with D3.js tree visualization showing full ancestral lineage, spouses rendered side-by-side, siblings, and extended family displayed
verification: Tab nav in header switches between Timeline and Family Tree views
Autocomplete input with dropdown suggestions for person selection
Keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown/Enter/Escape) in suggestions
D3 tree layout renders cards with name, dates, age, and role
Spouses render side-by-side at same level with dashed horizontal connector
Info bar shows ancestor, descendant, and sibling counts
Subject node highlighted with accent border and glow
Auto-scrolls to subject node on load

description: People's age shown alongside birth/death years in detail panel and timeline tooltips
verification: Detail panel formatLifespan() shows '(age X)' when both birth and death years known
Timeline tooltip appends age for people items
Age calculated as Math.abs(deathYear - birthYear)

description: Python build script (build-static.py) exports SQLite data to 7 JSON files and copies static assets to docs/ for GitHub Pages deployment
verification: python3 build-static.py runs without errors
docs/ contains index.html, css/styles.css, js/*.js, data/*.json
static/api.js provides client-side filtering, search, and lineage computation
test-static.py validates exported data (322 people, 89 events, 66 books)

description: docs/ has all JSON data embedded inline in index.html via window.__BIBLE_DATA__, enabling both file:// protocol and HTTP hosting
verification: docs/index.html contains <script>window.__BIBLE_DATA__={...}</script>
Opening docs/index.html directly in browser works without a server
static/api.js checks window.__BIBLE_DATA__ before falling back to fetch()

description: build-static.py builds docs/ with inline data from SQLite in a single step
verification: python3 build-static.py builds docs/
Data exported once from SQLite, inlined into index.html

description: Static site search uses client-side substring matching across names and descriptions instead of server-side FTS5
verification: static/api.js search() filters cached data with case-insensitive substring match
Search works identically in docs/ builds
Returns matching people and events with name, type, and description snippet

description: Static site computes lineage via BFS traversal of father_id/mother_id instead of server-side recursive CTEs
verification: static/api.js computeLineage() walks ancestors up via fatherId/motherId
Descendants found by scanning all people for matching parent IDs
Siblings found by shared fatherId or motherId
ExtendedFamily computed as children of ancestors not in other sets

description: Family tree person selection uses a single autocomplete text input with dropdown suggestions replacing the old search+select pattern
verification: Single #lineage-search input with #lineage-suggestions dropdown
Typing filters people by substring match, shows up to 20 results
ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigate suggestions, Enter selects, Escape closes
Each suggestion shows person name and date range

description: GitHub Actions workflow builds app, seeds database, exports static site, and deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main
verification: .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main branch
Steps: dotnet build → dotnet run → python3 build-static.py → deploy to Pages

description: Deterministic one-command bootstrap (bash init.sh): checks prerequisites, kills stale processes, restores packages, builds, runs tests, starts app
verification: bash init.sh completes without errors
Validates .NET 9+ SDK is installed
Runs dotnet test before starting app
App accessible at http://localhost:5180 after script completes

description: Health check dashboard (bash agent-status.sh) showing app status, API health, database state, and git status
verification: bash agent-status.sh completes without errors
Reports app running/stopped, API healthy/unreachable
Shows database file size and git branch/status

description: Quality audit script (bash quality-sweep.sh) checks architecture violations, build health, dead code, and debug artifacts
verification: bash quality-sweep.sh completes without errors
Checks for console.log/debugger statements in JS
Runs architecture tests and build verification

description: Python validation script (test-static.py) verifies exported static data integrity: item counts, people/event details, lineage people, periods, books
verification: python3 test-static.py passes all checks
Validates 416 timeline items (322 people, 94 events)
Validates 322 people details, 94 event details, 322 lineage people
Validates 11 periods and 66 books

description: Father of Lazarus placeholder person links Lazarus, Martha, and Mary of Bethany as siblings via shared father_id, enabling family tree rendering
verification: SeedData.sql inserts 'Father of Lazarus' with role='other'
Lazarus (123), Martha (124), Mary of Bethany (243) all have father_id pointing to Father of Lazarus
Family tree tab shows the Bethany family tree when searching for Lazarus
